summary: Normalizes a slash-delimited relative path by removing redundant separators and current-directory segments while resolving parent-directory segments according to a boolean policy.
signature_intent: Accepts a POSIX-style relative path string and a boolean that controls whether unresolved leading parent traversals are preserved; returns a normalized relative path string.
behavior: Uses forward slash as the only segment separator.
Resolves parent-directory segments by removing the preceding normal segment when possible.
Preserves unresolved parent-directory segments only when the boolean option permits it.
Returns a relative normalized string without preserving a trailing separator.
edge_cases: Empty input returns an empty string.
Inputs containing only separators or current-directory segments return an empty string.
Leading parent-directory segments are dropped when preservation is disabled.
Leading or excess parent-directory segments are retained when preservation is enabled.
Dot-prefixed names other than a single or double dot are treated as normal segments.
Parent traversal does not cancel a previously preserved unresolved parent segment.
forbidden_details: Low-level loop mechanics or character-code details.
